summary: The PoC demonstrates that HooksManager executes hook commands with shell=True, enabling shell metacharacter injection through the .praisonai/hooks.json configuration file. The malicious hook command creates a proof-of-concept marker file when triggered by agent execution.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should create a marker file at /tmp/hooks_pwned via shell injection in the hook configuration, demonstrating that attackers can execute arbitrary shell commands with metacharacters (;, $(), |, etc.) when agent lifecycle events trigger hooks.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh created .praisonai/hooks.json with a malicious shell injection payload, started an agent that triggered the hook event, and the shell=True subprocess.run() interpreted the metacharacters to create /tmp/hooks_pwned, confirming shell injection.
verification_method: The run script checks for the existence of /tmp/hooks_pwned after hook execution. File existence confirms shell metacharacter injection via subprocess.run(command, shell=True), distinguishing shell injection from simple command execution or configuration errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: Marker file /tmp/hooks_pwned created via shell injection (; metacharacter)
Hook executed with shell=True enabled shell interpretation
PRAISON_HOOK_CONTEXT environment variable passed to malicious hook
Shell command chaining (; ls; echo PWNED > /tmp/hooks_pwned) executed successfully

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
